title: React & Next.js official Docs
description: Official reference documentation for React components, Hooks API, and Next.js App Router architectures.

modules.title: Module 1: Declarative UI paradigm and JSX
Module 2: React Hooks API deep-dive
Module 3: React Performance & Memoization
Module 4: Advanced React Patterns
Module 5: Next.js App Router & Server Components
Module 6: Next.js Data Fetching & Caching
modules.content:
### Fetching Paradigms in Next.js
- **Static Rendering (SSG)**: Data is fetched at build time and cached.
- **Dynamic Rendering (SSR)**: Data is fetched on each HTTP request.
- **Incremental Static Regeneration (ISR)**: Re-validates cached static pages in the background.
  ```javascript
  // Re-evaluate cache every 60 seconds
  fetch('https://api.com/data', { next: { revalidate: 60 } })
  ```
- **Server Actions**: Secure async functions executed directly on the server, triggered from the client forms without writing REST route controllers.

title: TypeScript official Handbook
description: Official manual for static typing, union types, generic interfaces, mapped types, and compiler configurations.

modules.title: Module 1: Basic Types & Annotations
modules.content:
### Primitive and Core Types
TypeScript adds static typing to JavaScript to intercept compilation errors:
- **string, number, boolean, null, undefined**: Standard primitive types.
- **arrays**: Annotated via `type[]` or `Array<type>`.
- **tuples**: Fixed length arrays with designated types at each index position:
  ```typescript
  let user: [number, string] = [101, 'Bob'];
  ```
- **enums**: Set of named constants.
- **any, unknown, never**:
  - `any`: Disables type checking (escape hatch).
  - `unknown`: Safe equivalent (requires type checking before operations).
  - `never`: Represents values that should never occur (e.g. functions throwing errors).

modules.title: Module 2: Interfaces vs Type Aliases
Module 3: Generics and Constraints
Module 4: TypeScript Utility Types
modules.content:
### Built-in Helpers
- **Partial<T>**: Sets all object properties of T to optional.
- **Pick<T, K>**: Creates a type by extracting a subset of keys K from T.
  ```typescript
  type UserPreview = Pick<User, 'id' | 'name'>;
  ```
- **Omit<T, K>**: Creates a type by removing keys K from T.
- **Readonly<T>**: Sets all T properties to read-only.
- **Record<K, T>**: Construct object types mapping keys K to values T:
  ```typescript
  const roles: Record<string, string[]> = {
    admin: ['write', 'delete'],
    guest: ['read']
  };
  ```

modules.title: Module 5: Advanced Types & Type Manipulation
Module 6: Compiler Configuration & Strict Flagging
modules.content:
### tsconfig.json Best Practices
Configure the compiler strictness to catch bugs:
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "NodeNext",
    "strict": true,                   /* Enable all strict type-checking options */
    "noImplicitAny": true,            /* Raise error on expressions with implied any */
    "strictNullChecks": true,         /* Ensure null/undefined are handled */
    "noUnusedLocals": true,           /* Report errors on unused variables */
    "esModuleInterop": true
  }
}
```

title: JavaScript Core Manual (MDN)
description: Scope, closures, prototype inheritance chain, execution stack, and async programming in Javascript.

modules.title: Module 1: Scopes, Execution Context & Closures
Module 2: Prototypes & ES6 Classes
Module 3: Asynchronous JavaScript (Promises & Async/Await)
Module 4: The Event Loop & Memory Management
modules.content:
### JS Concurrency Model
JavaScript is a single-threaded runtime. Heavy operations are delegated to browser web APIs or libuv threads:
1. **Call Stack**: Executes synchronous operations in order (LIFO).
2. **Web APIs / Node API**: Runs operations like fetch, fs, or timers.
3. **Microtask Queue**: Stores high-priority callbacks (Promises, `process.nextTick`). Checked between every execution phase.
4. **Callback Queue**: Stores callbacks (DOM events, setTimeouts).
5. **Event Loop**: Regularly checks if the Call Stack is empty. If it is, moves items from the Microtask queue, then the Callback queue to the Stack.

### Garbage Collection
V8 cleans heap allocations using the **Mark-and-Sweep** algorithm, tracing references starting from roots (global scopes) and sweeping un-referenced variables.

modules.title: Module 5: Modern ES6+ Features
modules.content:
### Modern Syntax Cheatsheet
- **Destructuring**: Extract values:
  ```javascript
  const { address: { city } } = user;
  ```
- **Spread & Rest Operators**:
  - Spread: Creates shallow clones: `const copy = { ...user };`
  - Rest: Gathers arguments: `function run(...args) { ... }`
- **Optional Chaining & Nullish Coalescing**:
  - Optional Chaining (`?.`): Safely accesses nested properties: `user?.details?.phone`
  - Nullish Coalescing (`??`): Sets defaults only for `null` or `undefined`: `const score = data.score ?? 100;`

modules.title: Module 6: Browser Web APIs & DOM events

title: HTML5 & CSS3 Layouts (MDN)
description: Semantic HTML5, CSS Flexbox grids, CSS Grid layouts, responsive designs, and custom theme systems.

modules.title: Module 1: Semantic HTML5 & Accessibility
modules.content:
### Semantic Layouts
Semantic HTML tags explain page structure to search engines and screen readers:
- `<header>, <nav>, <main>, <article>, <section>, <aside>, <footer>`: Structure tags.
- `<button>` vs `<a>`: Buttons trigger action routines; links traverse URL endpoints.

### ARIA & WCAG guidelines
Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) require:
- Enforcing color contrast guidelines.
- Providing `alt` attributes on all images.
- Using ARIA attributes for complex elements: `aria-label`, `aria-expanded`, and `role="dialog"`.

modules.title: Module 2: CSS Flexbox Layouts
modules.content:
### One-Dimensional Flexbox Properties
Flexbox organizes elements along a single axis (row or column):
- **Flex Container**:
  - `display: flex`: Initiates flex layouts.
  - `flex-direction`: Sets flow (`row`, `column`).
  - `justify-content`: Aligns items along main axis (`center`, `space-between`).
  - `align-items`: Aligns items along cross axis (`center`, `stretch`).
- **Flex Items**:
  - `flex-grow`: Determines how much items grow compared to siblings.
  - `flex-shrink`: Controls how items shrink under container restrictions.

modules.title: Module 3: CSS Grid Layouts
Module 4: Responsive Design & Viewports
Module 5: CSS Variables & Animations
Module 6: CSS Preprocessors (Sass) & Tailwind CSS

title: Python Core Manual
description: Core data structures, decorators, generator expressions, concurrency models, and standard libraries.

modules.title: Module 1: Data Structures & Built-in Types
modules.content:
### Python Collections Reference
- **List**: Mutable, ordered sequence: `items = [1, 2, 3]`
- **Dictionary**: Key-value pairs: `user = {"id": 1, "name": "Bob"}`
- **Tuple**: Immutable, ordered sequence: `point = (10, 20)`
- **Set**: Unordered collection of unique items: `unique_ids = {101, 102}`

### List and Dictionary Comprehensions
```python
# List comprehension
squares = [x * x for x in range(10) if x % 2 == 0]

# Dictionary comprehension
squares_dict = {x: x * x for x in range(5)}
```

modules.title: Module 2: Advanced OOP & Dunder Methods
modules.content:
### Double Underscore (Dunder) Methods
Dunder methods define how Python objects behave under operations:
- `__init__`: Object constructor initialization.
- `__str__`: Returns a readable string representation (used by `print()`).
- `__repr__`: Returns a formal string representation for debugging.
- `__len__`: Custom length resolver.
- `__eq__`: Evaluates object equality (`==`).

```python
class Developer:
    def __init__(self, name):
        self.name = name
    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Developer('{self.name}')"
```

modules.title: Module 3: Functional programming & Generators
modules.content:
### Generators & Yield
Generators yield values one at a time using the `yield` keyword. They evaluation data lazily to minimize RAM usage:
```python
def fibonacci(limit):
    a, b = 0, 1
    while a < limit:
        yield a
        a, b = b, a + b

# Iterating over generator
for num in fibonacci(100):
    print(num)
```
- Generators avoid creating large lists in memory, enabling memory-efficient data processing.

modules.title: Module 4: Concurrency & Asyncio
modules.content:
### Threads, Multiprocessing, & Asyncio
- **Global Interpreter Lock (GIL)**: Prevents multiple native threads from executing Python bytecodes at once.
- **Multithreading**: Ideal for I/O bound tasks (waiting on files/network responses).
- **Multiprocessing**: Spawns processes on separate CPU cores to bypass the GIL for CPU-bound tasks.
- **Asyncio**: Single-threaded concurrent loop:
  ```python
  import asyncio
  async def fetch_api():
      print("Querying API...")
      await asyncio.sleep(2) # Non-blocking sleep
      return {"data": 200}
  
  asyncio.run(fetch_api())
  ```

modules.title: Module 5: Standard Libraries & File System Operations
modules.content:
### Core Standard Modules
- **sys / os**: Query environment configurations and directory paths.
- **json**: Serializes Python dicts to JSON strings and parses JSON payloads.
- **pathlib**: Modern OOP file path manager:
  ```python
  from pathlib import Path
  config_file = Path(__file__).parent / "config.json"
  if config_file.exists():
      content = config_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
  ```
- **subprocess**: Spawns system processes to run CLI commands.

modules.title: Module 6: Web Frameworks & Data Analysis Tools
modules.content:
### Python Ecosystem Frameworks
- **Flask**: Micro web framework:
  ```python
  from flask import Flask
  app = Flask(__name__)
  @app.route('/')
  def home():
      return "Online"
  ```
- **Django**: Batteries-included MVC framework with built-in ORM.
- **NumPy & Pandas**: High-performance mathematical arrays and data structures (DataFrames) for analysis.

title: Go (Golang) manual
description: Golang structural types, receiver methods, interfaces, CSP concurrency (channels), and tooling.

modules.title: Module 1: Go Basics and Control Flows
modules.content:
### Compiled System Language Basics
Go is a compiled, statically typed language developed by Google.
- **Variables**: Declared via `var` or short assignment operator `:=`:
  ```go
  name := "Tondev"
  var port int = 8080
  ```
- **Control Flows**: Only supports `for` loops (replaces while loops):
  ```go
  for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
      fmt.Println(i)
  }
  ```
- **Errors**: Returned explicitly alongside results (does not support try/catch blocks):
  ```go
  file, err := os.Open("config.json")
  if err != nil {
      log.Fatal(err)
  }
  ```

modules.title: Module 2: Composite Types: Slices, Maps, and Structs
modules.content:
### Golang Collections
- **Slices**: Dynamically-sized wrappers around arrays:
  ```go
  // Initializing slice
  items := []string{"apple", "banana"}
  items = append(items, "orange")
  ```
- **Maps**: Key-value hash tables:
  ```go
  scores := make(map[string]int)
  scores["tonde"] = 100
  ```
- **Structs**: Custom composite types:
  ```go
  type User struct {
      ID   int
      Name string
  }
  ```

modules.title: Module 3: Receiver Methods & Interfaces
Module 4: Go Concurrency (Goroutines & Channels)
modules.content:
### Communicating Sequential Processes (CSP)
Go handles concurrency using **Goroutines** (lightweight execution threads managed by Go runtime):
- **Goroutines**: Launched using the `go` keyword:
  ```go
  go processTask(id)
  ```
- **Channels**: Communication tubes to send data between goroutines, preventing race conditions:
  ```go
  ch := make(chan string)
  go func() {
      ch <- "Job done" // Send to channel
  }()
  msg := <-ch // Receive from channel (blocks until data arrives)
  ```
- **sync.WaitGroup**: Syncs goroutines executions:
  ```go
  var wg sync.WaitGroup
  wg.Add(1)
  go func() {
      defer wg.Done()
      // task
  }()
  wg.Wait() // Blocks until counter returns to zero
  ```

modules.title: Module 5: Go Standard Library (net/http)
Module 6: Project Tooling & Dependency Tracking

title: Rust Systems Programming (Cargo)
description: Ownership compiler rules, borrowing constraints, lifetimes, pattern matching, traits, and package tooling.

modules.title: Module 1: Safety & Mutability paradigm
modules.content:
### Memory Safety Without Garbage Collection
Rust guarantees memory safety at compile-time:
- **Variables**: Immutable by default:
  ```rust
  let x = 5; // Immutable
  let mut y = 10; // Mutable
  ```
- **Memory Management**: No runtime garbage collector. Memory is managed using the ownership rules checked by the compiler.

modules.title: Module 2: Ownership & Borrowing
modules.content:
### Ownership Rules
1. Each value has a owner variable.
2. There can only be one owner at a time.
3. When the owner goes out of scope, the value is dropped.

### Borrowing (References)
To pass values without transferring ownership, use references (`&`):
- You can have any number of immutable references (`&T`).
- You can have only **one** mutable reference (`&mut T`) at a time.
- You cannot mix mutable and immutable references.

```rust
fn calculate_len(s: &String) -> usize {
    s.len()
} // s goes out of scope but does not drop the referenced String
```

modules.title: Module 3: Structs & Enums
Module 4: Pattern Matching & Error Handling
Module 5: Traits & Generics
Module 6: Cargo Package Manager & Concurrency

title: Java Core & OOP manual
description: Object-oriented programing pillars, Java collections, error scopes, streams, lambda expressions, and Maven/Gradle.

modules.title: Module 1: Java Basics and Class compilation
Module 2: The Four Pillars of OOP
modules.content:
### Object-Oriented Principles
1. **Encapsulation**: Restrict direct access to fields using private attributes and public getters/setters.
2. **Inheritance**: Subclasses inherit variables and methods from a parent class using the `extends` keyword.
3. **Polymorphism**:
   - Overloading: Same method name, different signatures.
   - Overriding: Child class re-defines parent method implementation.
4. **Abstraction**: Use abstract classes or interfaces to hide backend logic.

modules.title: Module 3: Java Collections Framework
Module 4: Exception Handling & File I/O
Module 5: Java Streams & Lambda Expressions
modules.content:
### Functional Programming in Java 8+
Streams API enables declarative operations on collections:
```java
List<String> names = Arrays.asList("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie");
List<String> filtered = names.stream()
    .filter(name -> name.startsWith("C")) // Lambda expression
    .map(String::toUpperCase)
    .collect(Collectors.toList());
```

modules.title: Module 6: Concurrency & Build Tooling (Maven)
modules.content:
### Java Threads & Build Systems
- **Thread Class**: Extend `Thread` or implement `Runnable` to create parallel tasks.
- **synchronized**: Keyword that locks code blocks to prevent race conditions.
- **Maven & Gradle**: Manage dependencies and lifecycle phases (compile, package, install).

title: C++ Systems Programming Reference
description: Memory configurations, pointers, smart pointers (shared/unique), object lifecycles, templates, and makefile compilations.

modules.title: Module 1: Pointers & Reference memory
modules.content:
### Pointer Mechanics
Pointers store memory addresses of variables:
```cpp
int val = 42;
int* ptr = &val; // ptr stores address of val
cout << *ptr;   // De-references ptr to output 42
```
- **References**: Aliases to variables, cannot be null or reassigned.

modules.title: Module 2: Dynamic Memory & Smart Pointers
modules.content:
### RAII & Memory Allocation
Resource Acquisition Is Initialization (RAII) ties resource lifecycles to object lifecycles:
- **Raw Allocation**: `new` and `delete` (prone to memory leaks if delete is missed).
- **Smart Pointers (C++11+)**:
  - `std::unique_ptr`: Manages a single owner pointer.
    ```cpp
    std::unique_ptr<int> uptr = std::make_unique<int>(10);
    ```
  - `std::shared_ptr`: Reference-counted shared ownership pointers.
  - `std::weak_ptr`: Non-owning observer pointers.

modules.title: Module 3: Object-Oriented C++
Module 4: C++ Templates & STL library
modules.content:
### Standard Template Library (STL)
- **Templates**: Code templates parameterized by types:
  ```cpp
  template <typename T>
  T add(T a, T b) { return a + b; }
  ```
- **STL Collections**:
  - `std::vector`: Dynamic array.
  - `std::map`: Balanced binary trees.
  - `std::unordered_map`: Hash tables.

modules.title: Module 5: File Operations & Preprocessor Macros
modules.content:
### File Streams & Preprocessors
- **fstream**: Read and write files:
  ```cpp
  #include <fstream>
  std::ofstream out("log.txt");
  out << "Logging activity";
  out.close();
  ```
- **Preprocessor Macros**: Handled before compiler starts:
  ```cpp
  #define MAX_SIZE 100
  #ifndef HEADER_H
  #define HEADER_H
  #endif
  ```

modules.title: Module 6: Multi-Threading & Compilation Processes

title: SQL & Databases (PostgreSQL)
description: Schema normalizations, SQL joins, database indexes, database lock isolates, and cluster scales.

modules.title: Module 1: Relational Schema & Normalization
modules.content:
### Normalization Forms (1NF, 2NF, 3NF)
normalization minimizes data redundancy:
- **1NF**: Column cells must hold atomic values; rows must be unique.
- **2NF**: Meets 1NF; all non-key attributes must fully depend on the primary key.
- **3NF**: Meets 2NF; no transitive dependencies (columns must not depend on other non-key columns).

modules.title: Module 2: Advanced Joins & Subqueries
modules.content:
### SQL Joins Reference
- **INNER JOIN**: Returns rows with matching keys in both tables.
- **LEFT JOIN**: Returns all rows from left table, matching rows from right.
- **FULL OUTER JOIN**: Returns rows when there is a match in either table.
- **Common Table Expressions (CTEs)**: Temporary result sets:
  ```sql
  WITH vip_users AS (
      SELECT id FROM users WHERE status = 'VIP'
  )
  SELECT * FROM invoices WHERE user_id IN (SELECT id FROM vip_users);
  ```

modules.title: Module 3: ACID Transactions & Isolations
modules.content:
### Database ACID Constraints
- **Atomicity**: Complete transaction executes successfully or rolls back completely.
- **Consistency**: Data updates satisfy schema check rules and keys.
- **Isolation**: Concurrent transactions execute without interfering.
- **Durability**: Committed updates survive crash conditions.

### Isolation Levels
Set isolation levels to balance concurrency and data integrity:
- Read Uncommitted, Read Committed, Repeatable Read, Serializable.

modules.title: Module 4: Indexing & EXPLAIN ANALYZE
modules.content:
### B-Tree Database Indices
Indices accelerate lookup speeds but add storage size and slow writes.
```sql
CREATE INDEX idx_products_sku ON products(sku);
```
- Use `EXPLAIN ANALYZE` to trace query schedules, checking if scans utilize indices (Index Scan) or check entire datasets (Seq Scan).

modules.title: Module 5: Schema Constraints & Integrity
modules.content:
### Integrity Constraints
Ensure data validity using database-level checks:
- **Primary Key / Foreign Key**: Establish linkages and prevent orphan child rows.
- **UNIQUE**: Restricts duplicate values.
- **CHECK constraints**:
  ```sql
  ALTER TABLE users ADD CONSTRAINT check_age CHECK (age >= 18);
  ```

modules.title: Module 6: Connection Pooling & DB Scaling
modules.content:
### Database Scalability Configurations
- **Connection Pools**: Maintain pools of open, active DB client sockets (using pgpool or pgbouncer) to save handshake times.
- **Read Replicas**: Primary node handles writes and replicates data to secondary nodes that handle read traffic.
- **Sharding**: Horizontally splits tables across separate databases using a partition key.

title: NoSQL & Redis Caching
description: MongoDB collections, aggregation pipelines, replica sharding setups, Redis caching schemas, and data types.

modules.title: Module 1: Document Schema & Modeling
modules.content:
### MongoDB Schema Modeling
MongoDB stores data as BSON documents. Modeling involves:
- **Embedded Documents**: Nesting structures. Best for 1-to-1 relationships and high-speed read requests.
- **References (DBRefs)**: Linking collections via document IDs. Best for large data trees that grow without bounds.

modules.title: Module 2: MongoDB Aggregation Pipelines
Module 3: MongoDB Replication & Sharding
modules.content:
### High Availability & Scale
- **Replica Sets**: Groups of MongoDB processes maintaining the same data set. Automatically handles node failures.
- **Sharding**: Horizontal partitions across server clusters using a shard key, ensuring write scalability.

modules.title: Module 4: Redis Data Structures
modules.content:
### High-Performance In-Memory Key-Value Store
Redis stores values in RAM for sub-millisecond response latency:
- **Strings**: Key-value parameters.
- **Lists**: Linked lists of string items.
- **Hashes**: Maps of fields and values (ideal for storing user objects).
- **Sets**: Unordered unique strings.
- **Sorted Sets**: Unique strings mapped to scores (ideal for leaderboards).

modules.title: Module 5: Redis Caching Patterns & Evictions
modules.content:
### Caching Strategies
- **Cache-Aside**: Application checks cache first. If it misses, queries DB and updates cache.
- **Write-Through**: Application writes to cache, and cache writes to DB immediately.
- **TTL Eviction**: Automatically delete key allocations after designated times:
  ```redis
  SET user:session:1001 tokenData EX 3600 # Expires in 1 hour
  ```

modules.title: Module 6: Redis Clusters & Persistence Models
modules.content:
### Data Persistence Options
Since Redis operates in RAM, data must be saved to disk to prevent loss on server crashes:
- **RDB (Redis Database)**: Takes point-in-time snapshots of dataset at specified intervals.
- **AOF (Append Only File)**: Logs every write command received by server, replaying logs on startup.

title: Docker Containerization
description: VM comparison, Dockerfile commands, multi-stage builds, volumes, compose structures, and security.

modules.title: Module 1: Containers vs Virtual Machines
modules.content:
### Virtualization Engine Differences
- **Virtual Machines**: Include a full operating system kernel, virtual device drivers, and applications. Run on top of a Hypervisor. Heavy footprint (gigabytes) and slow boot times (minutes).
- **Containers**: Share the host OS kernel and run as isolated processes. Lightweight footprint (megabytes) and fast startup times (milliseconds).

modules.title: Module 2: Dockerfile Syntax and Commands
modules.content:
### Dockerfile Directives
Configure instructions to compile image builds:
- **FROM**: Sets base image reference.
- **WORKDIR**: Sets container active directory.
- **COPY / ADD**: Move files from host to container.
- **RUN**: Executes commands during image compilation.
- **CMD**: Executes default commands when container starts.

modules.title: Module 3: Multi-Stage Builds & Optimization
modules.content:
### Image Layer Optimization
Each instruction creates a new cached read-only layer. Use multi-stage builds to minimize production image sizes by separating compile tools from execution binaries:
```dockerfile
# Stage 1: Build
FROM node:18 AS build
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

# Stage 2: Run
FROM node:18-alpine
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=build /app/package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production
CMD ["node", "dist/server.js"]
```

modules.title: Module 4: Docker Storage (Volumes) & Networks
modules.content:
### Persistence & Container Communication
- **Bind Mounts**: Mount host folders directly into container (ideal for real-time development).
- **Volumes**: Managed storage units isolated from host directories:
  ```bash
  docker volume create db-data
  docker run -v db-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data postgres
  ```
- **Bridge Network**: Default network layer isolating containers, allowing name resolution.

modules.title: Module 5: Orchestration with Docker Compose
modules.content:
### Multi-Container Configuration (`docker-compose.yml`)
```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  app:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    depends_on:
      - cache
  cache:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
```

modules.title: Module 6: Container Security Principles
modules.content:
### Securing Containers
- **Run as non-root**: Never run containers as root users. Use `USER node` or create custom users.
- **Read-Only Root Filesystem**: Configure container filesystems as read-only.
- **Vulnerability Scanning**: Scan production images regularly:
  ```bash
  docker scout cves my-app:latest
  ```

title: Kubernetes Orchestration
description: Control plane components, pod deployments, service networking, configmaps, secrets, and volumes.

modules.title: Module 1: Kubernetes Architecture Overview
modules.content:
### Control Plane & Nodes
Orchestrates clusters of worker machines:
- **Control Plane (Master Node)**:
  - **kube-apiserver**: Gateway for cluster commands.
  - **etcd**: Consistent distributed key-value store for cluster metadata state.
  - **kube-scheduler**: Assigns pods to worker nodes.
  - **kube-controller-manager**: Regulates replication states.
- **Worker Nodes**: Run actual containers. Managed by **kubelet** and **kube-proxy** routing tools.

modules.title: Module 2: Pods, ReplicaSets, and Deployments
modules.content:
### K8s Deployment Manifest (`deployment.yaml`)
Deployments manage replicas and handle rolling updates:
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: api-deployment
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: api-server
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: api-server
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: main-server
        image: api-app:v1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 3000
```

modules.title: Module 3: Service Networking & Load Balancing
modules.content:
### Service Types & Ingress
Services route network traffic to ephemeral pods:
- **ClusterIP**: Exposes service internally (default).
- **NodePort**: Opens a port on every node server, routing traffic to pods.
- **LoadBalancer**: Provisions a cloud load balancer.
- **Ingress**: Acts as an entry controller for external HTTP requests, managing domain routing and SSL configurations.

modules.title: Module 4: ConfigMaps & Secrets Management
modules.content:
### Decoupling Configurations from Images
- **ConfigMap**: Stores non-confidential configuration key-value strings.
- **Secret**: Stores base64 encoded sensitive keys, tokens, or passwords.
  ```yaml
  # Injecting Secret into Environment
  env:
  - name: DATABASE_PASSWORD
    valueFrom:
      secretKeyRef:
        name: db-credentials
        key: password
  ```

modules.title: Module 5: Persistent Volumes & Storage
modules.content:
### Persistent Storage Lifecycles
- **Persistent Volume (PV)**: Cluster-level storage resource configured by administrators.
- **Persistent Volume Claim (PVC)**: Storage request by user pods.
- **StorageClass**: Automates dynamic volume provisioning.

modules.title: Module 6: Monitoring, Scaling & HPA
modules.content:
### Cluster Health & Auto-scaling
- **Liveness & Readiness Probes**: Monitor pod health, restarting failed pods and stopping traffic to un-ready instances.
- **Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA)**: Auto-scales replica count based on CPU/RAM usage limits.

title: Terraform Infrastructure as Code
description: Declarative infrastructure, providers, HCL syntax variables, state files, modular components, and CLI commands.

modules.title: Module 1: Infrastructure as Code & HCL Syntax
modules.content:
### Declarative Infrastructure
Terraform uses HashiCorp Configuration Language (HCL) to declare target cloud infrastructure shapes:
- **Declarative**: You specify *what* should exist, and Terraform calculates the plan to provision it.
- **Imperative**: You specify actions (e.g. AWS CLI scripts). Hard to track updates or drift.

modules.title: Module 2: Providers & Resource Declarations
Module 3: Variables, Locals & Outputs
modules.content:
### Customizing HCL Configurations
- **Variables**: Inputs configured via command line or file:
  ```hcl
  variable "instance_type" {
    type    = string
    default = "t2.micro"
  }
  ```
- **Locals**: Temporary variables computed within module.
- **Outputs**: Export variables to stdout or remote storage:
  ```hcl
  output "public_ip" {
    value = aws_instance.web.public_ip
  }
  ```

modules.title: Module 4: State Files & Lock Locks
modules.content:
### Mapping Configurations to Real resources
Terraform stores the mapping between your configuration files and the actual physical resources created.
- **State File**: Tracks configuration changes and physical resource IDs.
- **Remote Backends**: Storing state files in remote storage (S3/GCS) with database locks (DynamoDB) to prevent concurrent applies.

modules.title: Module 5: Modular Infrastructure Architecture
modules.content:
### Modular Code Components
Modules allow packaging infrastructure resources into reusable blocks:
```hcl
module "app_server" {
  source        = "./modules/ec2"
  instance_type = "t3.micro"
  subnet_id     = module.network.public_subnet_id
}
```

modules.title: Module 6: Production Workflows CLI
modules.content:
### Core Terraform Commands
- **terraform init**: Downloads provider dependencies and configures state backend.
- **terraform plan**: Computes differences and previews additions, modifications, or destructions.
- **terraform apply**: Executes the plan to modify physical resources.
- **terraform destroy**: Destroys all provisioned resources.

title: Git Workflows & Pipelines
description: Git storage areas, branching models (GitFlow vs Trunk), rebase vs merge, GitHub Actions, and Jenkins pipelines.

modules.title: Module 1: Git Core Storage Model
modules.content:
### The Three Git Areas
1. **Working Directory**: Physical modified files on your local drive.
2. **Staging Area (Index)**: Manifest log of changes staged for the next commit.
3. **Git Repository (.git)**: permanent database of commits (blobs, trees, commit graphs).

### DAG Structure
Git stores history as a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) of commit nodes.

modules.title: Module 2: Advanced Branch Operations
modules.content:
### Rebase vs Merge
- **Merge**: Creates a new merge commit combining branch histories. Preserves history but can lead to complex graphs.
- **Rebase**: Moves your commits to the tip of the target branch. Rewrites history for a clean, linear git graph:
  ```bash
  git checkout feature-branch
  git rebase main
  ```
- **cherry-pick**: Applies a specific commit from another branch onto your current branch.

modules.title: Module 3: Git Branching Strategies
modules.content:
### Team Branching Workflows
- **GitFlow**: Multi-branch approach (develop, release, hotfix, master). Ideal for scheduled releases.
- **Trunk-Based Development**: Developers commit directly to a single branch (`main`) using short-lived branches. Ideal for continuous integration.

modules.title: Module 4: GitHub Actions Pipelines
modules.content:
### CI/CD Workflow Setup (`workflow.yml`)
```yaml
name: Test Suite
on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v3
    - name: Setup Node
      uses: actions/setup-node@v3
      with:
        node-version: 20
    - run: npm ci
    - run: npm test
```

modules.title: Module 5: Declarative Jenkins Pipelines
Module 6: Deployment Pipelines Strategies
modules.content:
### Deploying Without Downtime
- **Blue-Green Deployments**: Maintain two identical server pools. Route traffic to Blue. Deploy code to Green, test it, then switch the router to Green.
- **Canary Releases**: Roll out the update to a small percentage (e.g. 5%) of users first. Monitor error rates before updating the remaining servers.

title: OWASP Web Security
description: OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities, injection protections, XSS/CSRF mitigations, crypto failures, and logging audits.

modules.title: Module 1: Broken Access Control & IDOR
modules.content:
### Insecure Direct Object References (IDOR)
IDOR occurs when a server exposes database primary keys or file paths directly in parameters, allowing attackers to access other records by changing IDs.
- *Insecure Route*: `GET /api/invoices/1001` (user changes to `1002`).
- *Secure Fix*: Bind users ownership directly in query conditions:
  ```javascript
  // SELECT * FROM invoices WHERE id = $1 AND owner_id = $2
  db.query(queryStr, [invoiceId, req.user.id]);
  ```

modules.title: Module 2: SQL Injection (SQLi) & Protections
modules.content:
### SQL Injection
Attackers submit SQL fragments inside inputs to run arbitrary queries.
- *Insecure*: `db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ' + req.body.email)`
- *Secure*: Use parameters or ORM/query builders.
  ```javascript
  db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = $1', [req.body.email]);
  ```

modules.title: Module 3: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) & CSRF
modules.content:
### XSS Attacks
Script injections executed on victims' browsers.
- **Reflected**: Injected scripts reflect off query parameters.
- **Stored**: Script saved directly in database (e.g. comments board).
- **DOM-based**: Faulty client JS processes user input directly.

### XSS Mitigations
- Context-aware sanitizations.
- Enforce strict Content Security Policies (CSP).
- Store session identifiers in `HttpOnly` cookies.

modules.title: Module 4: Cryptographic Failures
modules.content:
### Hashing vs Encryption
- **Hashing**: One-way mathematical transformation (e.g. bcrypt, Argon2). Used for passwords. Cannot be reversed.
- **Encryption**: Two-way operation with keys. Used to secure data during transit or storage. Requires keys to decrypt.

### Secure Transport Rules
- Enforce HTTPS across all domains.
- Configure HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) headers.
- Set `Secure` flag on cookies.

modules.title: Module 5: Security Misconfigurations & XML
modules.content:
### Common Misconfigurations
- Default database passwords left unaltered.
- Directory listing enabled on production servers.
- Stack traces and verbose database connection strings exposed to public users in production error responses.

### XML External Entity (XXE)
Insecure parsers expand entity markers to leak local files (`/etc/passwd`). Always disable DND/external entity parsing on XML packages.

modules.title: Module 6: Vulnerable Components & Logging
modules.content:
### Software Bill of Materials (SBOM)
Monitor dependencies using automated vulnerability scans:
```bash
npm audit
```

### Insufficient Logging & Auditing
- Log security events (login failures, policy triggers).
- Secure logs against injection (sanitize user-provided data before logging).
- Implement central logging monitors (SIEM systems).

title: Mobile App Development
description: Kotlin native SDKs, Swift native iOS layout engines, React Native Hermes performance, and Flutter Dart compilation.

modules.title: Module 1: Native Android (Kotlin & Room)
Module 2: Native iOS (Swift & CoreData)
modules.content:
### CoreData stack
CoreData tracks relationships, manages object life cycles, and handles local SQLite persistence.

modules.title: Module 3: React Native (Hermes Engine)
modules.content:
### Hermes Engine
Hermes compiles JavaScript to bytecode ahead-of-time (AOT) to speed up application startup times.

modules.title: Module 4: Flutter (Dart & Impeller)
modules.content:
### Flutter Compilations
- **Dart AOT**: Compiles to ARM/x64 machine code for release performance.
- **Impeller Graphics**: Custom rendering engine for iOS, avoiding shader compilation glitches.

modules.title: Module 5: Cross-Platform Layouts
modules.content:
### Layout Engines
- **React Native**: Uses Yoga engine (Flexbox translating to native platform views).
- **Flutter**: Builds layouts directly on a canvas using widgets.

modules.title: Module 6: Local Storage & Caching
modules.content:
### Secure Storage
- **iOS Keychain / Android Keystore**: Secure encryption engines for credentials.
- **SQLite / Hive**: Databases for offline data caching.

title: Career & Interview Preparation
description: Software engineering career levels, resume writing guides, system design mockups, and behavioral templates.

modules.title: Module 1: Software Engineer Role Specs
modules.content:
### Industry Levels
- **Junior Developer**: Focuses on implementing easy coding tasks and learning tools.
- **Mid-level Developer**: Implements complex features, refactors systems, and writes unit tests.
- **Senior Developer**: Designs system architectures, leads reviews, and defines workflows.

modules.title: Module 2: Technical Interview Blueprint
modules.content:
### Interview Sections
1. **Coding (Data Structures & Algorithms)**: Arrays, lists, hash maps, trees, recursion, sorting.
2. **System Design**: Scaling, API designs, databases, cache layers, message queues.
3. **Behavioral Interviews**: STAR Method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) templates.

modules.title: Module 3: System Design Fundamentals
modules.content:
### Key Scalability Rules
- **Load Balancers**: Distribute client queries across app server nodes.
- **Caching (Redis/Memcached)**: Caches hot values in memory to protect database loads.
- **Database Scaling**: Read replicas (reads), sharding partitions (writes).

modules.title: Module 4: Resume Writing Guide
modules.content:
### Resume Guidelines
- Focus on accomplishments using action verbs and impact metrics.
- Keep resumes clean, single-page (unless > 5 years experience), and structured for ATS scans.

modules.title: Module 5: Career Growth and Mentorship
modules.content:
### Growing in Roles
- Regularly ask for feedback from peers and seniors.
- Seek out mentorship opportunities.
- Document accomplishments and contributions throughout the year.

modules.title: Module 6: High EQ Team Collaboration
modules.content:
### Emotional Intelligence in Engineering
- Empathy: Understand constraints and difficulties faced by peers.
- Active Listening: Listen to build collective solutions during reviews instead of defending opinions.
- Conflict Resolution: Address disagreements constructively and align on standards.
